channel.title: BBC News
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
item.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

item.title: Trump says ICE agents will assist airport security as DHS shutdown continues
item.description: Travellers have faced hours-long waits at airports around the country as a partial government shutdown has left TSA agents without pay.

item.title: Hawaii storms have caused $1bn in damage, governor says
item.description: The full scale of the damages to roads and other infrastructure from rain and flooding in the state must still be assessed, Gov Josh Green says.

item.title: How a ban on religious symbols has triggered a Canadian constitutional debate
item.description: Quebec's law to regulate religious symbols goes to the Supreme Court in a constitutional test case.

item.title: 'We cannot be in peace', say Guthrie family as they renew appeal over missing mother
item.description: A seven-week nationwide search for 84-year-old Nancy Guthrie, the mother of TV anchor Savannah Guthrie has not identified any suspects.

item.title: The Bachelorette's messy break-up with its unlikely star Taylor Frankie Paul
item.description: The TikTok and reality TV star was a risky choice for the squeaky clean franchise - and ended up being a wrong one.

item.title: Robert Mueller, ex-FBI chief who led Trump-Russia investigation, dies at 81
item.description: A former FBI director, Mueller led the high-profile inquiry into alleged Russian interference in the 2016 US election

item.title: Bodycam footage of Justin Timberlake's 2024 drink-driving arrest released
item.description: The pop star's legal team had tried to prevent the release of footage from his arrest, but reached an agreement.

item.title: Thousands evacuated as Hawaii faces worst flooding in 20 years
item.description: "Don't take this storm lightly," Hawaii Governor Josh Green warned on Saturday, as more rain is expected on Oahu and Maui.

item.title: Pentagon restrictions on press violate First Amendment, judge rules
item.description: The Department of Defense had required reporters to agree to certain rules on what information they could gather in order to maintain access to the Pentagon.

item.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer actor Nicholas Brendon dies aged 54
item.description: Brendon died of "natural causes", his family said in a statement, remembering him as "passionate" and "sensitive".

item.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
item.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

item.title: Elon Musk misled Twitter investors, jury finds
item.description: A San Francisco jury concludes Musk’s claims in 2022 were damaging to a group of Twitter investors.

item.title: CBS News shuts down radio service after nearly 100 years
item.description: In a memo to staff, editor-in-chief Barri Weiss and president Tom Cibrowski say the decision is "necessary" but "not an easy one".

item.title: Trump threatens to send ICE into airports unless funding deal reached
item.description: The president said ICE would "do security" as airport security staff have gone without pay for weeks due to a partial government shutdown.

item.title: Mexican teen dies while being held in US immigration custody
item.description: Royer Perez-Jimenez died amid a surge of migrant deaths under Donald Trump's immigration crackdown.

item.title: Justice department seeks to dismiss charges against ex-officers over Breonna Taylor raid
item.description: The former officers were alleged to have used false information to obtain a search warrant to enter Breonna Taylor's home in 2020.

item.title: Are US and Israel aligned on Iran war? Deciphering Trump's post after gas field attacks
item.description: What does Trump's Truth Social post after gas field attacks tell us about US-Israeli alignment?

item.title: Nasa's Artemis Moon rocket rolls back to pad for possible April launch
item.description: Nasa is preparing a mission to send astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years.

item.title: Ros Atkins on... Trump's mixed messages on the war
item.description: The BBC’s Analysis Editor looks at President Trump’s mixed messages on the war with Iran.

item.title: Watch timelapse of Artemis II rocket rollout to launch pad
item.description: The 98m tall Space Launch System (SLS) and Orion spacecraft made a four mile journey from their assembly building to Pad 39B at the Kennedy Space Center in Florida.

item.title: Trump compares attack on Iran to Pearl Harbor in meeting with Japanese PM
item.description: In a meeting with Japanese Prime Minister, US President Donald Trump was asked why he didn't inform allies about his plan to attack Iran.

item.title: California leaders react to Cesar Chavez abuse allegations
item.description: The US civil rights leader has been accused of sexual abuse prompting Los Angeles and other communities to consider renaming places, schools, and streets named after Chavez.

item.title: Famous Iditarod dog sled race ends in repeat win for Jessie Holmes
item.description: The former reality TV star guided his dogs through a roughly 1,000-mile (1,609-kilometer) race, completing it in nine days, seven hours, and 32 minutes.

item.title: A secularism law some women say makes them feel like 'outsiders' heads to Canada's top court
item.description: Canada's Supreme Court will hear a challenge to Quebec's Bill 21, which bars some public sector workers, including teachers, from wearing religious symbols.

item.title: Remember Chuck Norris memes but never watched his films? You're not alone
item.description: Chuck Norris became an inadvertent source of humour for a viral online celebration of his movie tough-guy persona.

item.title: Five takeaways from Markwayne Mullin's Homeland Security confirmation hearing
item.description: The agency Mullin is vying to run entered its fifth week without funding, and concerns surrounding the budget loomed over questions.

item.title: Surge in US gas prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
item.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

item.title: Creed to Sinners: Michael B Jordan's road to Oscars recognition
item.description: The 39-year-old has spent more than two decades acting and picked up the award for best actor at the Oscars.

item.title: 'It's a tie, I'm not joking' - unusual Oscars moment sees two films share award
item.description: The rare but not unprecedented tie saw The Singers and Two People Exchanging Saliva both win for best short live action film.

item.title: Bespoke women's football stadiums? Why US sides are making the move
item.description: BBC Sport visits Kansas City Current and Denver Summit to learn why these US franchises are the first to build venues specifically for women's football.
